concerns.text: Assumption about multiplicity & power: 'a Bonferroni correction or similar family-wise error rate adjustment will be applied to the p-values'. The upstream idea explicitly asks to compare the measured slope against two specific theoretical predictions (photoevaporation vs. core-powered mass loss) to determine which is favored. Applying a Bonferroni correction to a two-hypothesis comparison in this context risks artificially inflating the threshold for significance, potentially obscuring the very distinction the research question seeks to resolve. The idea does not request a correction for multiple testing; it requests a direct comparison. This redefines the statistical inference goal from 'distinguish between two competing theories' to 'prove one theory is robust against a correction not requested by the hypothesis'.

FR-009 mandates a sensitivity analysis where the analysis 'PASSES if the absolute difference in gap location estimates between GMM and KDE is ≤ 0.05 R_earth'. The upstream idea and methodology sketch specify using KDE as a 'validation' method to ensure results are not artifacts of parametric assumptions, but do not define a specific numerical tolerance (0.05 R_earth) for passing. This introduces an arbitrary, unrequested success criterion that could cause the project to fail even if the scientific conclusion (slope comparison) is robust, simply because the two methods differ by 0.06 R_earth. This is over-reach on a specific engineering threshold not grounded in the research question.

Assumption about threshold justification: 'a sensitivity analysis sweeping these thresholds (e.g., -35 planets, 15-25% uncertainty) will be performed'. The upstream idea specifies the thresholds (<20% radius, <1% period, min 30 planets) as part of the methodology sketch. While sensitivity analysis is good practice, mandating a specific sweep range (-35 planets, 15-25%) in the spec constitutes over-reach. The idea does not request a sensitivity analysis of the filtering thresholds, only the analysis of the gap location itself. This adds significant computational work not implied by the core research question.
